TextENGNew: ``All right, class president, give us the signal.``
...It took me a moment to remember that the class president was me (Mion).
``Oh, uh, uh, uh! All rise!!``

``You seem a bit sleep-deprived yourself, class president.@k@vS06/22/151400003. These hot, muggy nights make it hard to rest.@k@vS06/22/151400004. But do make sure you're getting proper sleep.@k@vS06/22/151400005. Being well-rested is important!``
``Yeeees, ma'aam!``
...The atmosphere in this place, which seemed like a kindergarten and an elementary school mashed together and divided by three, was truly peculiar.
changeReason: Original omits the JPN metaphor of 'kindergarten and elementary school added together and divided by three'; new translation renders it faithfully.

TextENGNew: Charitably, you'd call it warm and harmonious.@kLess charitably... you'd question whether this even qualified as an educational institution.
changeReason: Original misplaces content about kindergarten/school blend (belongs in prior entry); JPN here contrasts 和気あいあい (harmonious) vs questioning if it's an educational institution.

TextENGNew: I'd gotten thorough advance information from Sis about the overall vibe, so nothing felt out of place.
The character Mion played at school was straightforward enough that it wouldn't be too hard to act the part.
Satoshi-kun was... just as Sis had described... utterly haggard.
When I greeted him, his response was practically indistinguishable from being ignored.
...Because I knew how cornered he was, both in body and spirit... it was agonizing to see.
But in this classroom, there was one other person who drew my interest besides Satoshi-kun.
changeReason: New translation adds 'besides Satoshi-kun' matching JPN 悟史くん以外に, clarifying the narrator's interest was in a second person.

TextENGNew: That was... Satoshi-kun's little sister, Satoko.
She looked every bit as ravaged as Satoshi-kun... like a doll that had lost all feeling.
Everyone in the class could see how painful their state was, yet no one had any means to help.@k...So the room was wrapped in an awkward pretense of not noticing.
Mion had given me only this one day.
...If I let myself zone out, a school day would be over before I knew it.
I had to figure out what I needed to accomplish in this limited window of time.
I felt I should... talk to Satoshi-kun about something.
But about what?
Anything would do.@k...Cheer him up, or sympathize with him, or anything.@k...I just wanted to say something to him.
But Sis had warned me beforehand.
...She said Satoshi-kun had been growing distant from Mion lately, and seemed to dislike her@kor at least wanted to keep his distance...
Approaching him carelessly might not go well.
...She understood my desire to talk to him, but... maybe it was better to leave him alone.@k...That was her counsel.
Given that Sis had issued that warning, I should have heeded it.
But... I chose to ignore it.
During lunch break, I followed Satoshi-kun as he left his seat, seemingly searching for a place to be alone, and called out to him in the hallway.
``...Satoshi-kun.``
Satoshi-kun flinched at my voice and turned around.
The expression on his face made me recoil.
...Because there, for the first time I'd ever seen, was a look of unmistakable rejection.
But... I pressed on without faltering.
...Because if I hesitated now, this day -- this single opportunity -- would be wasted.
Satoshi-kun fixed me with a cold stare that seemed to ask: 'Does this look like someone who's been fine?'
...I never imagined Satoshi-kun would look at me with eyes like those.
The pain was so sharp it was almost unbelievable...
As I began to lower my gaze, Satoshi-kun spoke first.
...I felt a small flicker of relief, but the words that followed cut me again.
``...I'm fine.@k@vS06/07/151200144. ...Anything else?``
``...If not, I'm going.@k@vS06/07/151200146. ...I want to be alone during lunch.``

``R-Right...@k@vS06/06/150600620. ...Your part-time jobs must be exhausting.@k@vS06/06/150600621. Of course you'd want some time alone to rest...@k@vS06/06/150600622. Hey, um... where are you working today?``
Sis had been the one to arrange Satoshi-kun's jobs.
It wasn't the same place each time. He was doing fill-in shifts at whatever shop or workplace needed temporary help -- a different job practically every day.
Which meant Satoshi-kun was constantly thrown into unfamiliar work in unfamiliar settings.
And because the sum he needed was so large, he'd specifically requested the highest-paying jobs available.
High-paying part-time work meant... heavy lifting, manual labor, that kind of thing.@k...For Satoshi-kun, who'd never been physically robust, it was grinding him down day by day.
``...Does it matter?@k@vS06/07/151200148. ...Besides, shouldn't you already know where I'm working? You're the one who set it up, Mion...``
...Mion was the one who'd arranged the work.@k...Yet there wasn't a shred of gratitude in Satoshi-kun's tone.
...This wasn't the Satoshi-kun I knew.@k...It was deeply... heartbreaking.
``...Hahaha, you're right.@k@vS06/06/150600625. ...Sorry.``
``...Is that all...?``
Satoshi-kun's rejection pierced my heart like a blade.
changeReason: Original reduces to 'He sounded harsh'; JPN 拒絶が胸に突き刺さる (rejection pierced my chest) is a visceral metaphor the new translation captures.

TextENGNew: ...Why... was he being so cold?
Was the Satoshi-kun who gently stroked my head... gone...?
Emotion spilled from my eyes, just a little.
A single hot streak traced down my cheek.
But even seeing that, Satoshi-kun only showed a momentary flicker of hesitation before his expression hardened back into displeasure.
``...Oh, s-sorry...@k@vS06/06/150600628. Um... the coach says... he'd really like you to come back to practice...@k@vS06/06/150600629. Ahaha... just passing on the message.``
``...I already told the coach I want to quit the team.@k@vS06/07/151200151. ...I'm not going back.``
``B-But, you know...@k@vS06/06/150600631. ...It'd be good to cut loose and work up a sweat sometimes.@k@vS06/06/150600632. You know, sports can be really good for--``
``Not interested.``
I couldn't understand the irrational hostility being hurled at me, blow after blow.
...I couldn't fathom why I was being treated with such contempt... All I could do was flounder.
``...Because I'm busy with work. Don't ask me things you already know.@k@vS06/07/151200154. ...It's annoying.``
changeReason: New translation includes 'Don't ask me things you already know. It's annoying' matching JPN lines the original omits.

TextENGNew: ``...That's not what I mean!!@k@vS06/06/150600636. Why do you have to treat me like this?!``
``...Why don't you ask your own heart about that?``
``My own heart?!@k@vS06/06/150600637. What?!@k@vS06/06/150600638. What did I do?!@k@vS06/06/150600639. I don't understand, Satoshi-kun!!``
My emotions erupted, and I could no longer contain them.
Hot streaks from both eyes... layering, streaming, pouring down.
...Satoshi-kun gave me a look of utter annoyance -- as though looking at something tediously unpleasant -- then turned on his heel and walked away without another word.
I... couldn't even call after him.
...If I had, even colder blades of words would have come flying back, piercing my chest further.
...I couldn't bear any more of that.@k...All I could do was watch his retreating back.
Wiping my tears, I started back toward the classroom.
That's when I heard crying from inside.
...I wasn't startled exactly, but I peered into the classroom to see what was happening.
...The one crying was... Satoko.
Her bento box lay scattered on the floor.
A classmate told me that after finishing her meal, Satoko had stood up to wash her bento box when she bumped into someone and dropped it.@k...That was all.
And yet over something that trivial... Satoko was bawling as though terrified of receiving a terrible scolding.
It wasn't as if she'd dropped some precious vase and shattered it beyond repair.
...Pick up the bento box from the floor, and that would be the end of it.
...The latch on the lid seemed to have come loose, but it was the kind of thing you could snap back into place with a click.@k...This was nothing to wail about.
And yet... through her tears, begging for forgiveness, Satoko screamed:
``Help me, help me... Nii-nii!!@k@vS06/04/150400002. Waaaaahhhhhhh!!!``
My emotional gates were already on the verge of breaking, and I felt the fire of fury that had kindled in my heart engulf my entire being.
changeReason: Original simplifies to 'couldn't suppress emotions'; JPN describes fire of fury engulfing her entire being, which the new translation preserves.

TextENGNew: ...This was an explosion of emotion that was beyond my control.
I shouldered aside the classmates who were hovering uncertainly around the sobbing Satoko, my heels striking the floor as I stormed toward her.
Standing before Satoko as she wailed without restraint, I...
...as if hurling my uncontrollable feelings at her, shoved her hard against the wall.
Satoko let out a short scream, her face a blank of incomprehension.
Tears streaming, body trembling.@k...She couldn't understand why I was directing this violence at her.
``...Do you understand why this is happening to you?``
changeReason: Different framing: original says 'why I did that' (speaker-focused) but JPN 何であんたがそういう目に遭うか means 'why this is happening to you' (victim-focused).

TextENGNew: ``Eeee... hh... h-help... Nii-nii...``
Satoko made no attempt to comprehend my question. She just cried out those words again.
I grabbed her by the collar again and flung her, this time sending her tumbling across the floor.
Satoko crashed gracelessly into a desk, knocking it over.@kIts contents scattered everywhere -- an ugly mess.
Satoko didn't try to stand.@kShe curled up tight, looking up at me with wide, trembling eyes.
``Help... help me... Nii-nii... Nii-nii...``
``It's because you're like this...!!!``
...It's because you cling to Satoshi-kun so easily... can't you see that he's suffering because of you?!
That's what I wanted to scream, but my throat, parched with rage, couldn't produce the words.
I snatched up a bundle of textbooks from the floor and hurled them at her pathetic, pleading face.
``Go ahead and cry if you want!!@k@vS06/06/150600643. But crying won't solve a single thing!!``
``Why are you crying?!``
``Because you think someone will come save you if you do?!``
``You have no idea how deeply the person who saves you gets hurt in your place, do you?!!!``
``Do you even understand?!``
``Do you understand your sin?!``
changeReason: Original says 'what you're doing' but JPN あんたの罪 means 'your sin/crime'; new translation correctly uses 'sin.'

TextENGNew: ``It'd be better if you didn't exist!!``
``If you're suffering that much, then just die!!``
``Go curl up and die by yourself!!!``
changeReason: Original uses 'Go fuck yourself' but JPN ヘソでも噛んで勝手に死んじゃえばいい is an idiom meaning 'go curl up and die alone'; new is more accurate.

TextENGNew: ``Stop making Satoshi-kun suffer!!``
``Suffer alone and die alone!!``
``Just die already, you worthless--!!!``
``Waaaaahhhhhhh!!!``
``Nii-nii!! Nii-nii!!! Whaaaaahhhh!!!``
Satoko's wailing was nothing but fuel on my fire now.

``Don't think crying will fix everything!!!@k@vS06/06/150600656. Don't think clinging to your brother will make it all go away!!@k@vS06/06/150600657. Stop being so helpless!!!@k@vS06/06/150600658. Grow up!!``
changeReason: New translation omits the JPN lines お前さえいなければ ('if only you didn't exist') present in the original.

TextENGNew: I kept scooping up textbooks and notebooks from the floor and hurling them at Satoko.
...Satoko curled into a ball like a turtle retreating into its shell, and screamed even louder.
A girl with long hair leapt forward and threw herself over the cowering Satoko.
...I knew who this was. She was famous enough.
...Furude Rika.@kOne of Satoko's few friends.
``...Please don't hurt Satoko.@k@vS06/05/150500002. Satoko has been through so much. Please, you mustn't hurt her...``

``You think being pitiable gives her a free pass to do anything?!@k@vS06/06/150600661. You think being pitiable makes it okay to lean on Satoshi-kun?!@k@vS06/06/150600662. You're just enabling her!!``
``It's because she never stops being helpless... that Satoshi-kun has to suffer so much!!!``
If Rika was going to shield Satoko, then she too became a target of my fury.
``I'm trying to teach this spoiled girl a lesson!!!@k@vS06/06/150600665. Get in my way and I'll crack your head open first!!``
I grabbed a chair and raised it overhead.
Being struck by something like this wouldn't end with just a bruise.
Rika squeezed her eyes shut and pressed herself over Satoko, offering her own body as a shield.
I no longer cared.
I was going to bring it down on both of them.
Rena, who'd sensed something terribly wrong and rushed back to the classroom, hurled her voice at me as I stood with the chair raised high.
And pushing past Rena, charging into the room, was... Satoshi-kun.
Satoshi-kun seemed to grasp Satoko's situation in an instant.
...And he came at me in a dead sprint, tackling me.
I was still stunned, not understanding what had happened, until my body slammed into the locker.
The locker buckled with a tremendous crash.@k...I doubled over from the unfamiliar impact.
``Satoko!!@k@vS06/07/151200158. Are you okay, Satoko...?!``
Satoko shoved aside Rika -- who just moments ago had been shielding her with her own body -- and threw herself at Satoshi-kun, crying.
``I didn't... @k@vS06/04/150400009_2. I didn't do anything, I didn't do anything!!@k@vS06/04/150400009_3. Mion-san... Mion-san just suddenly...@k@vS06/04/150400009_4. Waaaaahhh!``
``Wh-What is the meaning of this, Mion?!!!``
Satoshi-kun clutched his sobbing sister's head tight against him and screamed at me.@k...His face was terrifying.
``What I meant was... I just...``
``What did Satoko do?!@k@vS06/07/151200168. What did we ever do to you?!@k@vS06/07/151200169. Why do we always have to be bullied?!@k@vS06/07/151200170. Why?!``
He released Satoko, closed the distance between us, and grabbed me by the collar.
``Earlier... you told me to ask my own heart, didn't you?@k@vS06/06/150600668. ...Then why don't you try asking Satoko's heart?``
changeReason: Critical difference: original says 'ask yourself' but JPN 沙都子の胸に聞いてみりゃ means 'ask Satoko's heart'; new translation corrects this.

TextENGNew: ``Ask her WHAT?!!!!``
``You've admitted it yourself, haven't you, Satoshi-kun?@k@vS06/06/150600670. ...Satoko clings to you so completely that she's become your burden, hasn't she?!``
``If Satoko were stronger, you wouldn't have to go through any of this!!@k@vS06/06/150600672. She's the root cause of everything!!!``
Maybe I'd hit the nail on the head.
changeReason: Old says 'I don't believe what I said was wrong' (confident assertion) while JPN 図星だったのかもしれない means 'maybe I hit the nail on the head' (speculative observation about striking a nerve). Different framing and certainty level.

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun's reaction to those words was explosive beyond anything I'd anticipated.
``You... you...!!!@k@vS06/07/151200174. Don't you dare say that!!!``
changeReason: Original says 'What do you know?' but JPN いい加減なことを言うんじゃない means 'don't say irresponsible/reckless things'; new translation is closer.

TextENGNew: ``Ow... owowowowowow...!!!``
Satoshi-kun seized my hair and swung me around, then hurled me against the wall -- exactly as I'd done to Satoko.
But this time, I didn't back down either.
...The dam inside me had already completely collapsed. My emotions were in full flood.
``You're too soft on Satoko, Satoshi-kun!!!@k@vS06/06/150600675. It's because you keep coddling her without limits that she'll never stop clinging to you!!!``

``What do you know about us?!@k@vS06/07/151200176. What could you possibly know?!@k@vS06/07/151200177. Your people drove our parents into a corner... and bullied them relentlessly!!@k@vS06/07/151200178. And it didn't end with them!!``

``You keep tormenting anyone branded a village traitor, never letting up!!@k@vS06/07/151200181. Is it that much fun for you?!@k@vS06/07/151200182. Is bullying the weak really that entertaining?!@k@vS06/07/151200183. What did we ever do to you?!!``
``STOP IT ALREADYYYYY!!!!``
The last to scream was Rena.
With a face as fierce as a demon's, glaring alternately at me and Satoshi-kun, she forced herself between us.
``Satoshi-kun, please stop. That's enough.@k@vS06/02/150200129. Mii-chan didn't mean any harm.``
``...She wanted to help you, and her feelings just got tangled up and spun out of control.@k@vS06/02/150200131. ...Right?``
Satoshi-kun looked as though he wanted to say something back, but in the end, he said nothing.
``You too, Mii-chan. That's enough.@k@vS06/02/150200133. ...I know nobody worries about Satoshi-kun more than you do.``
``But this isn't the way to fix things.@k@vS06/02/150200135. If you think about it calmly for even a moment, you know that, don't you?``
Since Satoshi-kun chose silence, I decided to hold my tongue as well.

``Come on, Satoko-chan, you too.@k@vS06/02/150200137. There's no need to cry over a dropped bento box.@k@vS06/02/150200138. Okay?@k@vS06/02/150200139. See? The latch just needs to be clicked back into place, and it's as good as new.``
``...Satoko, I'll pick up your bento for you...``
Rika, unable to bear Satoko's pain, began gathering up the scattered contents of her bento box from the floor.
``Everyone... can you help clean up?@k@vS06/02/150200141. The teacher will be back from the curry garden any minute now.@k@vS06/02/150200142. The fight's over, so let's not worry her, okay?``
changeReason: Original says 'vegetable garden' but JPN カレー菜園 specifically means 'curry garden'; new translation corrects this detail.

TextENGNew: The class exchanged glances... then gave small nods and began picking up what had been strewn across the floor.
``There.@k@vS06/02/150200145. Satoshi-kun, Mii-chan -- shake hands and make up.@k@vS06/02/150200146. ...Come on.``
``.........Sorry...@k@vS06/06/150600677. ...I didn't mean for this to happen...``
The last fragments of my emotional storm... spilled from my eyes once more.
``...I'm sorry too.@k@vS06/07/151200186. ...I know our family's troubles have nothing to do with you, Mion...@k@vS06/07/151200187. ...I shouldn't have taken it out on you.``
``Good. Now, Mii-chan, next you apologize to Satoko-chan.@k@vS06/02/150200148. ...Go on.``
...I could apologize to Satoshi-kun. But I had absolutely no intention of apologizing to Satoko.
Still, the situation wouldn't settle without it... so I reluctantly tossed some vague words of apology in Satoko's direction.
Satoko gave a sullen nod, signaling she accepted...
changeReason: Original says 'discouraged' but JPN 憮然 means 'sullen/disgruntled'; different emotional register.

TextENGNew: Released by Rena at last... I pressed both hands against my throbbing head.
...What was that...?@kWhat did I just do...?!
I didn't get this precious time just to pick fights...!
Sis had warned me, hadn't she...?
She'd told me Satoshi-kun had an unapproachable air about him, that it was better not to talk to him.
What... did I even come here for?
...If this was how it was going to turn out, I'd have been better off not coming at all.@k...Stupid, stupid... you idiot, Shion.
I'd exploded because I was thinking of Satoshi-kun's wellbeing.
And yet somewhere it went wrong -- no, maybe it had been wrong from the very start.@k...And I'd ended up in the absurd outcome of fighting with the very person I cared about.
Regret.
Self-loathing.
...No amount of self-reproach could undo what I'd done.
My world had gone pitch black.
...If the sky was going to fall, I wished it would hurry up and crush me...
Sis seemed caught between exasperation and sympathy -- a complicated mix.
``...I'm already swamped with the Watanagashi preparations...@k@vS06/03/150300135. and you had to go and make things even more complicated...``
Sis started to say something, but seemed to realize that saying it now would be pointless, and swallowed the words.
...After enduring a few choice complaints, I reported every detail of what had happened.
Sis would have to own the trouble I'd caused as something she herself had done... so her annoyance was entirely understandable.
``...So I won't get another chance to switch... for a while...?``
``...Shion... I wish you'd spare a thought for the trouble I'm in...``
``...Yeah... you're right...``
Even Sis, who'd been relatively sympathetic up to this point... finally let her voice turn bitter.
After stirring up this much chaos, asking for another switch would naturally meet with reluctance.
An uncomfortable silence stretched between us...
``...Hypothetically... if you did get one more chance,@k@vS06/03/150300138. ...what would you do, Shion?``
...A perfectly reasonable question.
...But I hadn't prepared an answer.
Suppose I could go to school again and see Satoshi-kun.@k...What would I even do...?
Apologize for today?
...No matter what approach I took, no matter how I phrased it.@k...With Satoshi-kun in his current state, anything I said would only backfire.
...That's right.@k...I remembered something Satoshi-kun had said.
...Wasn't it the Sonozaki family that had relentlessly persecuted the Houjou family?
For Satoshi-kun to despise anyone bearing the Sonozaki name was... entirely natural.
...But... then why only now?
...I'd been a Sonozaki to him the entire time.
Satoshi-kun had stroked my head knowing full well he believed me to be Sonozaki Mion...
``...Sis.@k@vS06/06/150600682. Honestly... Satoshi-kun does resent the Sonozaki family... doesn't he?``
``...I... think so, yes.``

There was no question that the Houjou family had been bullied by the entire village, made into a scapegoat for the sake of unity.@kAnd there was equally no question that the Sonozaki family had led, directed -- incited, even -- that persecution.
changeReason: Original omits significant JPN detail about village-wide bullying and scapegoating for unity; new translation renders the full content.

TextENGNew: Even after the dam war ended, while the overt bullying may have stopped, the atmosphere in the village had remained deeply hostile toward them.

Their family's life had been destroyed,@kand they'd been driven into their current wretched circumstances -- if Satoshi-kun traced all of that back to the Sonozaki family@kand harbored resentment, it wasn't misplaced in the slightest.
``But...@k@vS06/06/150600684. ...if that's the case... why now?@k@vS06/06/150600685. ...Why is he only showing it now?``
``...Why... is this happening now...?@k@vS06/06/150600687. If that's how he felt... if that was always underneath...@k@vS06/06/150600688. ...uuuuhh...!@k@vS06/06/150600689. ...ooh...!``
If he'd looked at me with those rejecting eyes from the very first time we met...
...I would never have tried to get close to him.
I'd forgotten to wipe the hot tears streaming from both eyes.
...It was only logical for Satoshi-kun to resent the Sonozakis.@kThere was nothing unnatural about it.
Rather.@k...What was unnatural was... why had he been kind until now?@k...Why had he treated me normally?@k...I knew questioning that was itself absurd...
``...I think the only answer is... that Satoshi was... mature enough to separate those things...``
He'd treated me normally because he was mature enough to do so -- even though I was a Sonozaki?
...But now... because the strain on his body and mind had become too great... the resentment buried deep inside had erupted... and he'd begun to reject me...?
No matter what angle I approached it from, no matter how I turned it over.@k...I couldn't find any fault in Satoshi-kun for my anguish.
If there was anyone to blame... it was someone I'd never once directed my thoughts toward until now.
``...Why... did she decide to persecute the Houjou family in the first place...?!``
``It was Satoshi-kun's parents who supported the dam, right?!@k@vS06/06/150600695. Satoshi-kun never supported it!@k@vS06/06/150600696. Why persecute the entire family?!``
Mion was at a loss, unable to form a reply.
Even without Sis spelling it out... I had a general sense of the answer.
It wasn't that the Sonozaki main house had issued an explicit order to bully the Houjou family.
The old hag had simply 'thought' the Houjou family was displeasing.
That displeasure was read as an unspoken will, and duly carried out.
But the grandmother knew perfectly well how her words propagated, how they rippled outward, what effects they would produce.

Which meant it wasn't merely 'thinking' -- it was nothing less than an explicit command.@k...The grandmother -- the Sonozaki main house -- had ordered the insidious campaign against the Houjou family.

``You once said the Furude priest wasn't being mature because he wouldn't cooperate, right, Sis?@k@vS06/06/150600699. So it's the same thing here?@k@vS06/06/150600700. Anyone who disrupts harmony has to be punished?``
changeReason: New translation truncates the comparison about the Houjou family not being 'mature' for disrupting harmony, which the original includes from JPN.

TextENGNew: ``And that's why sanctions were necessary!@k@vS06/06/150600702. That's how it works, isn't it?!``
Mion could no longer even muster a response.
I understood that my accusation had hit dead center.
``Then the sanctions should have been enough for the Houjou parents alone!!@k@vS06/06/150600704. Drag them into the main house's secret room and chop off a finger or something!!``
``But that should have been the end of it, shouldn't it?!@k@vS06/06/150600706. Satoshi-kun has nothing to do with this!!@k@vS06/06/150600707. He is completely innocent!!``
``What did Satoshi-kun ever do?!@k@vS06/06/150600709. What is his crime?!@k@vS06/06/150600710. Answer me!!@k@vS06/06/150600711. Answer me, Sis!!``
``...Very well.@k@vS06/03/150300145. I'll leave it in your hands, then.@k@vS06/03/150300146. Excuse me.``
Mion's voice turned suddenly cold and formal, and she hung up the phone, cutting me off mid-sentence.
For an instant, I thought she'd hung up to escape my interrogation, and I felt a volcanic surge of fury.
I started to redial the main house number,@k...then remembered that her abrupt, formal sign-off was our pre-arranged signal for 'the grandmother is coming, I'm cutting the call.'
...Whether the grandmother had actually appeared was debatable.
But since she'd invoked our agreed protocol, I couldn't call back now.
I headed to the sink, wanting to cool my overheated head.
I turned the faucet. What came out was unpleasantly lukewarm water.
No matter how much of it I splashed on my face, it did absolutely nothing to change my mood.
